---
title: Protein Database Description
subtitle: SpringBloom2021 - Genome-resolved Metaproteomic Dataset
date: 09.04.2025
author: Cyrill Hofer
output:
  rmdformats::readthedown:
    highlight: tango
    self_contained: true
    code_folding: hide
    thumbnails: true
    gallery: false
    fig_width: 15
    df_print: kable
    fig_caption: yes
    use_bookdown: true
    css: custom.css
---

# Data Preparation
- Note: This data does not contain and protein identifications from contaminants
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
# Clear R's Brain
rm(list = ls())

# Set the working directory
setwd("/SynologyDrive/PhD/Projects/SpringBloom2021/DataAnalysis_SpringBloom2021/Supplementary/SpringBloom_Metaproteomics/PeptidesProteinsRecovered/")

# Load the libraries
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(DT)
library(plotly)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(forcats)
library(patchwork)
library(data.table)

# Load the color palette
source("ColorPalette.R")

# Load the data
dat <- read_csv(file = "PeptideProteinRecovery_Summary.csv")
Protein_Distribution <- read_csv(file = "PeptideProteinDistribution.csv")
```


```{r}
# 1) Recode & order Stage
dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    Stage = recode(Stage,
      Raw      = "Unfiltered",
      `Q<1%`   = "1% Peptide FDR",
      `All<1%` = "1% Protein FDR",
      `2+ pep` = ">2 Peptides Per Protein"
    ),
    Stage = factor(Stage, levels = c(
      "Unfiltered",
      "1% Peptide FDR",
      "1% Protein FDR",
      ">2 Peptides Per Protein"
    ))
  )

# 2) Build long-form counts
bar_data <- bind_rows(
  dat %>% transmute(Stage, Metric = "Peptides", Category = "Proteotypic",     Count = ProteotypicPeptides),
  dat %>% transmute(Stage, Metric = "Peptides", Category = "Non-proteotypic", Count = Peptides - ProteotypicPeptides),
  dat %>% transmute(Stage, Metric = "Proteins", Category = "Proteotypic",     Count = ProteotypicProteins),
  dat %>% transmute(Stage, Metric = "Proteins", Category = "Non-proteotypic", Count = Proteins - ProteotypicProteins)
)

# 3) Compute percentages & mid-points once
anno <- bar_data %>%
  group_by(Metric, Stage) %>%
  mutate(
    Total = sum(Count),
    Pct   = Count / Total * 100,
    Cum   = cumsum(Count),
    Mid   = Cum - Count/2
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 4) Colors (shared)
fill_cols <- c(
  "Proteotypic"     = ColorPalette[2],
  "Non-proteotypic" = ColorPalette[4]
)

# 5) Helper to build each panel
make_panel <- function(metric, breaks, limits) {
  df <- filter(bar_data, Metric == metric)
  an <- filter(anno,      Metric == metric)
  
  ggplot(df, aes(Stage, Count, fill = Category)) +
    geom_col(color = "black", stroke = 0.1) +
    geom_text(
      data = an,
      aes(y = Mid, label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Pct)),
      color    = "black",
      fontface = "bold",
      size     = 4
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = breaks,
      limits = limits,
      labels = comma
    ) +
    labs(title = metric, x = NULL, y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}

# 6) Create the two panels
p_pep <- make_panel("Peptides", seq(0,  90000, 10000), c(0, 95000))
p_prt <- make_panel("Proteins", seq(0,  40000,  5000), c(0, 40000))

# 7) Combine with one shared legend + global title
p <- (p_pep + p_prt) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Proteome Search Quality Filtering",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    )
  ) & 
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols) &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# 8) Print (and you can ggsave it as before)
print(p)
```

```{r}
# --- PEPTIDE & PROTEIN LENGTH DISTRIBUTIONS — already long‐form ---------------

# Order Phylum by mean protein length
phylum_order <- Protein_Distribution %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(mean_prot = mean(Protein_Length, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_prot)) %>%
  pull(Phylum)

PD <- Protein_Distribution %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels = phylum_order))

# Total counts for subtitles
n_peptides <- nrow(PD)
n_proteins <- PD %>% distinct(Protein_ID) %>% nrow()

# Per‐phylum peptide counts + labels
phylum_pep_counts <- PD %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(n_peptides = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(Facet_Label = paste0(Phylum, " (n=", n_peptides, ")"))

PDp <- PD %>%
  left_join(phylum_pep_counts, by = "Phylum") %>%
  mutate(Facet_Label = factor(Facet_Label, levels = phylum_pep_counts$Facet_Label))

# Compute density peaks and means for peptides
peptide_means <- PDp %>%
  group_by(Facet_Label) %>%
  summarise(
    Peptide_Mean = mean(Peptide_Length, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_Y        = max(density(Peptide_Length)$y),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Label_Y = Max_Y * 1.1,
    Label_X = Peptide_Mean + 0.3
  )

# Peptide length density plot
p_pep_len <- ggplot(PDp, aes(x = Peptide_Length, fill = Phylum)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6, adjust = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(data = peptide_means,
             aes(xintercept = Peptide_Mean),
             linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_text(data = peptide_means,
            aes(x = Label_X, y = Label_Y, label = round(Peptide_Mean, 1)),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = -0.5, size = 2.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Facet_Label, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(6, 31), breaks = seq(6, 30, by = 2)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ColorPalette) +
  labs(title = paste("Peptide length distribution (7–30 aa) — Total N =", n_peptides),
       x = "Peptide length (aa)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

# Per‐phylum protein counts + labels
phylum_prot_counts <- PD %>%
  distinct(Phylum, Protein_ID, Protein_Length) %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(n_proteins = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(Facet_Label = paste0(Phylum, " (n=", n_proteins, ")"))

PDpr <- PD %>%
  distinct(Phylum, Protein_ID, Protein_Length) %>%
  left_join(phylum_prot_counts, by = "Phylum") %>%
  mutate(Facet_Label = factor(Facet_Label, levels = phylum_prot_counts$Facet_Label))

# Compute density peaks and means for proteins
protein_means <- PDpr %>%
  group_by(Facet_Label) %>%
  summarise(
    Protein_Mean = mean(Protein_Length, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_Y        = max(density(log10(Protein_Length))$y),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Label_Y = Max_Y * 1.1,
    Label_X = Protein_Mean * 1.03
  )

# Protein length density plot (log10 scale)
p_prot_len <- ggplot(PDpr, aes(x = Protein_Length, fill = Phylum)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6, adjust = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(data = protein_means,
             aes(xintercept = Protein_Mean),
             linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_text(data = protein_means,
            aes(x = Label_X, y = Label_Y, label = round(Protein_Mean)),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = -0.5, size = 2.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Facet_Label, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(0, 30, 100, 300, 1e3, 3e3, 1e4),
                labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ColorPalette) +
  labs(title = paste("Protein length distribution (log10) — Total N =", n_proteins),
       x = "Protein length (aa, log10)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

# Print and export
print(p_pep_len)
print(p_prot_len)

ggsave("Plot_Peptide_LengthDistribution_In_Database.png",
       plot   = p_pep_len,
       device = png,
       height = 21, width = 29.7,
       units  = "cm", dpi = 300)

ggsave("Plot_Protein_LengthDistribution_In_Database.png",
       plot   = p_prot_len,
       device = png,
       height = 21, width = 29.7,
       units  = "cm", dpi = 300)

```

```{r}
# 1) Summarise per species ---------------------------------------------------
species_summary <- Protein_Distribution %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(Proteins = n_distinct(Protein_ID),
    Peptides = n_distinct(Peptide_ID),
    KEGG_Classified = n_distinct(KEGG_ID[!is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified"]),
    .groups = "drop")

# 2) Totals & maxima for label placement ------------------------------------
phylum_totals <- species_summary %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(total_proteins = sum(Proteins),
    total_peptides = sum(Peptides),
    total_kegg_classified = sum(KEGG_Classified), .groups = "drop")

# compute 5%‐above maxima
global_max_prot <- max(species_summary$Proteins,        na.rm = TRUE)
global_max_pep  <- max(species_summary$Peptides,        na.rm = TRUE)
global_max_kegg <- max(species_summary$KEGG_Classified, na.rm = TRUE)

phylum_totals <- phylum_totals %>%
  mutate(y_prot = global_max_prot * 1.05,
         y_pep  = global_max_pep  * 1.05,
         y_kegg = global_max_kegg * 1.05)

overall_proteins <- sum(phylum_totals$total_proteins)
overall_peptides <- sum(phylum_totals$total_peptides)
overall_kegg     <- sum(phylum_totals$total_kegg_classified)

# 3) Order Phyla by median Proteins (for consistent x‐axis) -----------------
phylum_order <- species_summary %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(med = median(Proteins), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(med)) %>%
  pull(Phylum)

species_summary <- species_summary %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels = phylum_order))

phylum_totals <- phylum_totals %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels = phylum_order))

# 4) Color mapping ------------------------------------------------------------
fill_cols <- setNames(ColorPalette, phylum_order)

# 5) Build each plot ---------------------------------------------------------

# a) Tryptic peptides per species
p_pep <- ggplot(species_summary, aes(Phylum, Peptides)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = Phylum), width = 0.35, shape = 21, colour = "black", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Phylum), width = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3, colour = "black") +
  geom_text(data = phylum_totals, aes(x = Phylum, y = y_pep, label = total_peptides), size = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 3000, by = 500), limits = c(-1, 3000)) +
  labs(title = paste0("Detected Peptides per species  (total=", overall_peptides, ")"), x = NULL, y = "Peptides [n]") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14))

# b) Proteins per species
p_prot <- ggplot(species_summary, aes(Phylum, Proteins)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = Phylum), width = 0.35, shape = 21, colour = "black", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Phylum), width = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3, colour = "black") +
  geom_text(data = phylum_totals, aes(x = Phylum, y = y_prot, label = total_proteins), size = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 600, by = 50), limits = c(-1, 600)) +
  labs(title = paste0("Detected Proteins per species  (total=", overall_proteins, ")"), x = NULL, y = "Proteins [n]") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14))

# c) KEGG classified proteins per species
p_kegg <- ggplot(species_summary, aes(Phylum, KEGG_Classified)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = Phylum), width = 0.35, shape = 21, colour = "black", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Phylum), width = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3, colour = "black") +
  geom_text(data = phylum_totals, aes(x = Phylum, y = y_kegg, label = total_kegg_classified), size = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 400, by = 50), limits = c(-1, 400)) +
  labs(title = paste0("KEGG classified proteins per species  (total=", overall_kegg, ")"), x = "Phylum", y = "KEGG IDs [n]") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14))

# 6) Stack vertically & add main title --------------------------------------
final_plot <- (p_pep / p_prot / p_kegg) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 1.2)) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Proteome Overview", theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18, hjust = 0.5)))

print(final_plot)

# 7) Save to PDF -------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave("Per_phylum_database_summary.png",
       plot   = final_plot,
       device = png,
       width  = 14, height = 7,
       units  = "in", dpi = 300)


```


```{r}
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Full script: From data summarization (step 1) to plotting and saving
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------

# 0) Define phylum order and color palette --------------------------------
phylum_order <- c(
  "Actinomycetota",   "Bacteroidota",     "Pseudomonadota",
  "Verrucomicrobiota","Planctomycetota",  "Chloroflexota",
  "Bdellovibrionota", "Acidobacteriota",  "Armatimonadota",
  "Myxococcota",      "Nitrospirota",     "Thermoproteota",
  "Patescibacteria",  "Gemmatimonadota",  "Cyanobacteriota",
  "Deinococcota",     "Chlamydiota"
)

names(ColorPalette) <- phylum_order


# 1) Summarise per species ---------------------------------------------------
species_summary <- Protein_Distribution %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Proteins        = n_distinct(Protein_ID),
    Peptides        = n_distinct(Peptide_ID),
    KEGG_Classified = n_distinct(KEGG_ID[!is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified"]),
    .groups         = "drop"
  )

# Global median proteins per species
global_median_prot <- median(species_summary$Proteins, na.rm = TRUE)

# 2) Totals per phylum & label positions ------------------------------------
phylum_totals <- species_summary %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(
    total_proteins        = sum(Proteins),
    total_peptides        = sum(Peptides),
    total_kegg_classified = sum(KEGG_Classified),
    .groups               = "drop"
  )

# Compute label height
global_max_prot <- max(species_summary$Proteins, na.rm = TRUE)

# 3) Identify Top-8 phyla by total proteins ---------------------------------
Top8_Phyla_Most_Number_Of_Proteins <- phylum_totals %>%
  arrange(desc(total_proteins)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 8) %>%
  pull(Phylum)

ordered_top8 <- phylum_order[phylum_order %in% Top8_Phyla_Most_Number_Of_Proteins]

# 4) Create Phylum_Concatenated ---------------------------------------------
species_summary <- species_summary %>%
  mutate(
    Phylum_Concatenated = if_else(
      Phylum %in% ordered_top8,
      Phylum,
      "Rest"
    ),
    Phylum_Concatenated = factor(
      Phylum_Concatenated,
      levels = rev(c(ordered_top8, "Rest"))
    )
  )

# 5) Build phylum_totals2 including “Rest” row -------------------------------
rest_total <- phylum_totals %>%
  filter(!Phylum %in% ordered_top8) %>%
  summarise(
    total_proteins = sum(total_proteins)
  ) %>%
  mutate(Phylum = "Rest")

phylum_totals2 <- phylum_totals %>%
  filter(Phylum %in% ordered_top8) %>%
  bind_rows(rest_total) %>%
  mutate(
    Phylum_Concatenated = factor(
      Phylum,
      levels = rev(c(ordered_top8, "Rest"))
    )
  )

# Species count per displayed group for labels above boxplots
species_counts <- species_summary %>%
  group_by(Phylum_Concatenated) %>%
  summarise(
    n_species = n_distinct(Species_ID),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    y_lab = 620   # slightly above plotting range; adjust if needed
  )

# 6) Color mapping -----------------------------------------------------------
fill_cols <- c(
  ColorPalette[ordered_top8],
  Rest = "red"
)

# 7) Build the plot ----------------------------------------------------------
p_prot_horiz <- ggplot(
  species_summary,
  aes(
    x    = Phylum_Concatenated,
    y    = Proteins,
    fill = Phylum_Concatenated
  )
) +
  # global median line
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = global_median_prot,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    linewidth  = 0.8,
    colour     = "black"
  ) +
  # jittered points
  geom_jitter(
    width  = 0.35,
    height = 0,
    shape  = 21,
    colour = "black",
    size   = 2.5,
    alpha  = 0.7
  ) +
  # boxplots
  geom_boxplot(
    width         = 0.75,
    outlier.shape = NA,
    alpha         = 0.7,
    colour        = "black"
  ) +
  # species count labels above each boxplot
  geom_text(
    data = species_counts,
    aes(
      x = Phylum_Concatenated,
      y = y_lab,
      label = paste0("n = ", n_species)
    ),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    trans   = "sqrt",
    breaks  = c(0, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 400, 600),
    limits  = c(0, 650),   # increased upper limit so labels are visible
    expand  = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proteins Per Species [n]"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 12, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(size = 12)
  )

# 8) Display the plot -------------------------------------------------------
print(p_prot_horiz)

# 9) Export -----------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = "Proteins_Per_Species.png",
  plot     = p_prot_horiz,
  width    = 12,
  height   = 4,
  units    = "in",
  dpi      = 300
)
```
